text.title: Prompt Caching Rules and Monitoring
text.summary: Rules for structuring prompts to maximize cache hit rate, monitoring cache effectiveness, and avoiding the most common cache-busting mistakes.

rule.statement: Place the most stable content first in the system prompt and mark it with cache_control: ephemeral. Put dynamic content last.
rule.rationale: Cache uses prefix matching. Any change before the cached block invalidates the entire cache. Stable content at the top maximizes reuse.

rule.statement: Never inject dynamic values (timestamps, request IDs, user names) before the cached block.
rule.rationale: A single character difference in the prefix causes a cache miss. You pay the write cost on every call with no read benefit.

rule.statement: The minimum cacheable prefix is 1024 tokens; do not mark shorter content as cacheable.
rule.rationale: Prefixes below 1024 tokens are not eligible for caching. Marking them wastes the ephemeral annotation without any cost reduction.

rule.statement: Log cache_read_input_tokens from usage on every cached call. Zero means cache miss.
rule.rationale: Cache misses are silent — the API does not error; it just charges full input token cost. Monitoring the metric is the only way to detect misconfigurations.

rule.statement: Target cache_read_input_tokens / input_tokens ratio above 0.70 for meaningful cost benefit.
rule.rationale: Cache write tokens cost 25% more than regular input. Below ~70% hit rate the write overhead starts to erode savings.

section.title: TTL Behavior
section.p: Cache TTL is 5 minutes. Each cache hit extends the TTL by 5 minutes. Cache is org-scoped — shared across all API keys in the organization.

example.note: hit_rate is logged for each call to detect cache configuration issues.

antipattern.description: Injecting a request timestamp or user ID into the system prompt before the cached block.
antipattern.reason: Every call produces a unique prefix, guaranteeing 0% cache hit rate. You pay write cost on every request with no savings.

text.reference: Prompt Caching wrapper with cache hit rate logging
